title: Fix libpod API conformance to swagger
body:
* Return empty array when nothing has been pruned.
* Use correct return type swagger doc-comment.

resolves #12112 #12114

comments.body: /cc @jwhonce
@arctic-alpaca try this out.

Prune differs slightly from the swagger doc.
Expected:
```
[
   {
      "error":"string",
      "id":"string",
      "space":0
   }
]
```
Actual:
```
[
   {
      "Id":"a3fd813d4f58f7358734751ee69bd605e1da1e877ab8ef083edd10a111fbefc8",
      "Err":null,
      "Size":5869682
   }
]
```

~Err being `null` instead of `""` is part of the 4.0 API changes, isn't it?~

~I understand, that the changes to the API won't be back-ported to 3.4 but can the swagger doc for 3.4 be corrected to match the behavior in 3.4?~
@arctic-alpaca I am not changing API, I am rather changing swagger doc.
@matejvasek I might be a bit tired, thanks for the clarification.
LGTM
/approve

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *matejvasek*, *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I am tring also omit empty value for `Err`, so there is no `"Err":null,` in response.
```go
type PruneReport struct {
	Id   string `json:"Id"` //nolint
	Err  error  `json:"Err,omitempty"`
	Size uint64 `json:"Size"`
}
```

But I am seeing something REALLY REALLY weird.

when I run:
```go
package reports

import (
	jsoniter "github.com/json-iterator/go"
	"os"
	"testing"
)

var json = jsoniter.ConfigCompatibleWithStandardLibrary

func TestName(t *testing.T) {

	pr := []*PruneReport{&PruneReport{Id: "myid", Size: 42}}

	coder := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
	coder.SetEscapeHTML(true)
	coder.Encode(pr)
}
```
from  `pkg/domain/entities/reports/prune_test.go` (new file created by me).
The error is omitted as expected.

When I run the same code from `pkg/api/handlers/utils/handler_test.go`
The error is not omitted! WTF?

Same applies for endpoint handler, the error filed is not omitted despite the fact it is omitted using json tag.
if this is an API handler, then we usually use `schema:...` rather than JSON @matejvasek I am not sure if this is causing your issue but it could impact what is read properly.

> It might be some package thing. Because following code does work in handler package:
> 
> ```go
> func TestName(t *testing.T) {
> 	pr := []*reports.PruneReport{&reports.PruneReport{Id: "myid", Size: 42}}
> 	_ = pr
> 
> 	x := struct {
> 		A string `json:",omitempty"`
> 		B string `json:"B"`
> 	}{ }
> 	coder := jsoniter.ConfigCompatibleWithStandardLibrary.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
> 	coder.SetEscapeHTML(true)
> 	coder.Encode(x)
> }
> ```

that might work because they are of type string, I think the type error might be causing your issue. Try changing whether or not `Err` is a pointer to an error or the value at that pointer and see if anything works
@cdoern You are right about string vs. error type.
@jwhonce why we have custom error writer set up? Why aren't we omitting error?
@arctic-alpaca is `"Err":null` causing you some troubles that would be fixed by `"Err":""`?
@baude I added two commits PTAL

> @baude I added two commits PTAL

@baude sorry for tagging, I meant @mheon

> 
> 
> @arctic-alpaca is `"Err":null` causing you some troubles that would be fixed by `"Err":""`?

@matejvasek For values that are absent, it's actually nicer for me to have `"Err":null`.
@arctic-alpaca would it be problem if it was omitted? I like that more than `""`.

@matejvasek You mean instead of `"Err":null` just no `"Err"` field in the response json? Without clear markings in the API docs which fields can be omitted, this can lead to confusion whether something is missing because it's not relevant / not set or if it's missing because of a bug. Functionally, omitting fields doesn't cause issues for me.
@matejvasek Thanks for your PR!
@jwhonce is this really breaking change? Only thing that could cause issues is omitting a filed `Err,omitempty` I could revert it. But I think most of code can handle omitted filed.
I think that this shouldn't break contract between `podman` service and `podman-remote`.
It's more of a doc change.
@jwhonce I added a test as you requested.
/lgtm
@jwhonce It's probably late now but I would rather disabled the `Err` omission so it can be back-ported to 3.X.
and event with the omission I think most client should be able that. There is no API change with exception of omission of `Err` filed, instead of using explicit `null`.
@matejvasek Can the part where prune returns `null` instead of an empty array be backported to 3.x?

@arctic-alpaca this issue was marked as `braking-change` so this will be possibly in `podman` `4.0`.

But I am not sure if this is really breaking change. There are two changes to API but they are really subtle and IMHO majority of clients should work with it (at least official `Go` libpod would).

The changes are:
* Omitting error if it's `nil` instead of putting explicit `null` in output JSON.
* Returning empty array JSON `[]` instead of `null` JSON.

BTW @arctic-alpaca I noticed you don't like `null` much and you would prefer something else but IMHO it's perfectly valid for optional values to be `null`. OK for arrays maybe `[]` is better, but for strings I would prefer `null` (or omission) to `""`.

> 
> 
> BTW @arctic-alpaca I noticed you don't like `null` much and you would prefer something else but IMHO it's perfectly valid for optional values to be `null`. OK for arrays maybe `[]` is better, but for strings I would prefer `null` (or omission) to `""`.

@matejvasek I think there has been a misunderstanding, I completely agree with you on `null` being better than `""` for strings or generally returning `"field":null` or omission for empty fields. Where I don't think `null` is a good choice is if it's the only thing that's being returned. I would prefer `{}` for empty objects and `[]` for empty arrays.

I mostly had issues with discrepancies between the swagger docs and what actually got returned or `null` being all that's returned and not knowing whether this is intended or not.

 If fields that can be `null` or can be omitted would be marked in the swagger docs, that would greatly increase the confidence in a specific returned `null` being intended.
@arctic-alpaca the `breaking-change` label has been removed so this might end up in some 3.x release latter.
